0: Creating an __init__.py file within the fastmcp_client_agent directory is necessary. This
1: ensures the agent is recognized as a discoverable Python package for the ADK.
2: To begin, open a new terminal and run `python fastmcp_server.py` to start the
3: FastMCP server. Next, go to the parent directory of `fastmcp_client_agent` (for
4: example, `adk_agent_samples`) in your terminal and execute `adk web`. Once the
5: ADK Web UI loads in your browser, select the `fastmcp_greeter_agent` from the agent
6: menu. You can then test it by entering a prompt like "Greet John Doe." The agent will
7: use the `greet` tool on your FastMCP server to create a response.
9: What: To function as effective agents, LLMs must move beyond simple text
10: generation. They require the ability to interact with the external environment to access
11: current data and utilize external software. Without a standardized communication
12: method, each integration between an LLM and an external tool or data source
13: becomes a custom, complex, and non-reusable effort. This ad-hoc approach hinders
14: scalability and makes building complex, interconnected AI systems difficult and
16: Why: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) offers a standardized solution by acting as a
17: universal interface between LLMs and external systems. It establishes an open,
18: standardized protocol that defines how external capabilities are discovered and used.
19: Operating on a client-server model, MCP allows servers to expose tools, data
20: resources, and interactive prompts to any compliant client. LLM-powered applications
21: act as these clients, dynamically discovering and interacting with available resources
22: in a predictable manner. This standardized approach fosters an ecosystem of
23: interoperable and reusable components, dramatically simplifying the development of
25: Rule of thumb: Use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) when building complex,
26: scalable, or enterprise-grade agentic systems that need to interact with a diverse and
27: evolving set of external tools, data sources, and APIs. It is ideal when interoperability
28: between different LLMs and tools is a priority, and when agents require the ability to
29: dynamically discover new capabilities without being redeployed. For simpler
